etymology.description: semantic 士(士) (shì, "scholar") + phonetic 爿(爿) (pán) -> strong
etymology.interpretation: The character combines the semantic 士(士) (shì, "scholar"), associated with authority or prominence, and the phonetic component 爿(爿) (pán), which provides the sound, to convey the idea of strength or robustness, emphasizing a strong or sturdy stature.

parts.english: bed; half of a tree trunk

examples.chinese: 他看起来很强壮(他看起來很強壯)。
这栋建筑很壮观(這棟建築很壯觀)。
经过锻炼，他变得更加壮实(經過鍛煉，他變得更加壯實)。
examples.pinyin: Jīngguò duànliàn, tā biàn dé gèngjiā zhuàngshí.
examples.english: After exercising, he became even stronger.
